questions.question: Explain why √2 is irrational using contradiction.
questions.answer: Assume √2 = p/q in lowest form. Squaring gives 2q² = p², implying p is even. Let p = 2k, then q is also even, contradicting p and q being coprime. Hence √2 is irrational.

questions.question: Explain why every composite number can be written as a product of primes.
questions.answer: By repeatedly factorising a composite number, we ultimately reach prime factors. This is guaranteed by the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic.

questions.question: Why does HCF × LCM formula not hold for three numbers?
questions.answer: The relation HCF × LCM = product holds only for two numbers and fails when extended to three numbers.

questions.question: Prove that √3 is irrational.
questions.answer: Assume √3 = p/q. Squaring gives 3q² = p², implying p and q both divisible by 3, contradicting coprime condition. Hence √3 is irrational.

questions.question: Explain why Euclid’s Division Algorithm is valid for all positive integers.
questions.answer: Because repeated division produces smaller non-negative remainders until zero is reached.

questions.question: Why is √8 irrational?
questions.answer: √8 = 2√2 and since √2 is irrational, √8 is also irrational.

questions.question: Explain why terminating decimals depend on factors 2 and 5.
questions.answer: Because denominators with only 2 and 5 as prime factors produce powers of 10.

questions.question: Explain the importance of uniqueness in prime factorisation.
questions.answer: It ensures that no number has more than one prime factor structure, avoiding contradictions.

questions.question: Why is √49 rational but √50 irrational?
questions.answer: √49 = 7, an integer, while √50 is not a perfect square and is irrational.

questions.question: Explain why the sum of a rational and irrational number is irrational.
questions.answer: If the sum were rational, it would contradict the definition of irrational numbers.

questions.question: Why does p | a² imply p | a?
questions.answer: Because prime factorisation of a² contains each prime factor of a twice.

questions.question: Explain why Euclid’s algorithm always ends in finite steps.
questions.answer: The remainders form a decreasing sequence of positive integers.

questions.question: Why is 2 the only even prime number?
questions.answer: All other even numbers are divisible by 2 and hence composite.

questions.question: Explain why √5 is irrational.
questions.answer: Assuming √5 rational leads to both numerator and denominator divisible by 5, a contradiction.

questions.question: Why are coprime numbers important in irrationality proofs?
questions.answer: They help establish contradictions when common factors appear.

questions.question: Explain why √7 is irrational.
questions.answer: Assuming √7 rational leads to a contradiction using prime factorisation.

questions.question: Why is prime factorisation called fundamental?
questions.answer: Because it forms the basis of many results in number theory.

questions.question: Explain why irrational numbers cannot be expressed as decimals that repeat.
questions.answer: Repeating decimals can always be written as fractions, which contradicts irrationality.

questions.question: Why is √1 rational?
questions.answer: √1 = 1, which is an integer and hence rational.

questions.question: Why does HCF of two prime numbers equal 1?
questions.answer: Prime numbers have no common factors other than 1.

questions.question: Explain why √12 is irrational.
questions.answer: √12 = 2√3 and since √3 is irrational, √12 is also irrational.

questions.question: Why does contradiction invalidate an assumption?
questions.answer: Because a true assumption cannot lead to a false result.

questions.question: Explain why Euclid’s algorithm helps find HCF.
questions.answer: Because the last non-zero remainder is the HCF.

questions.question: Why are real numbers necessary?
questions.answer: They represent all possible points on the number line.

questions.question: Why does irrational × rational remain irrational?
questions.answer: Because the irrationality cannot be eliminated by multiplication.
